userStory: As a guest user, I want the Home page to display guest-state controls and guest-accessible feature buttons, so that I can use the application before login and open the Login page from the Person/Profile button.
WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins at: http://localhost/washtabui/home The Home page is the shared landing page for both unauthenticated and authenticated users.
This workflow validates only the guest-state behavior of the existing Home page before login.
The guest state is not a separate page from the authenticated Home state.
The application is launched at http://localhost/washtabui/home - The user is unauthenticated and has not signed in - The Home page loads in guest state
Initial Home page load in guest state
Visibility and interactivity of approved guest-state controls
Visibility and interactivity of shared Home shell controls
Navigation from the Person/Profile button to the Login page or Login view

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home page loads successfully in guest state from the approved entry URL
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page loads successfully at http://localhost/washtabui/home in guest state with guest-accessible controls visible and no authenticated user state displayed.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Person/Profile button is visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Person/Profile button is visible, enabled, clickable, and displayed as part of the Home page guest-state controls.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Back button is visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Back button is visible and enabled while the application remains in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Notification button is visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Notification button is visible and enabled while the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button is visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home button is visible and enabled while the application is in Home page guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Customer signup button is visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Customer signup button is visible, enabled, and available for guest access.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Clean survey button is visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Clean survey button is visible, enabled, and accessible to unauthenticated users.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Site survey button is visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Site survey button is visible, enabled, and accessible in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation from Person/Profile button opens Login page or Login view
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application transitions from Home guest state to the Login page or Login view without authenticating the user.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify return to Home guest state after opening Login view from Person/Profile button
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns to the same Home page guest state with guest controls visible and without displaying authenticated user controls.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Customer signup button navigates to customer-search page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application navigates from the Home guest state to the customer-search page while the session remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Clean survey button navigates to vehicle-survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application navigates from the Home guest state to the vehicle-survey page without requiring login.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Site survey button navigates to survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application navigates from the Home guest state to the survey page while remaining in unauthenticated mode.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button interaction preserves guest-state session
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application remains on or returns to the Home page guest state without authenticating the user or hiding guest controls.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Notification button interaction does not expose authenticated functionality to guest users
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Notification button does not expose authenticated-only functionality and the application remains in guest-accessible state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Back button interaction does not incorrectly authenticate the guest user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application does not enter an authenticated state and no logged-in user controls become visible after using the Back button.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest-accessible controls remain available after returning from customer-search page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page displays the Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button as visible and enabled after returning.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest-accessible controls remain available after returning from vehicle-survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page returns to guest state with all approved guest-accessible controls visible and enabled and without authenticated user indicators.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest-accessible controls remain available after returning from survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page guest state is restored with approved guest controls accessible and authenticated-only controls still absent.

validations: The Home page loads successfully at http://localhost/washtabui/home in guest state with guest-accessible controls visible and no authenticated user state displayed.
The Person/Profile button is visible, enabled, clickable, and displayed as part of the Home page guest-state controls.
The Back button is visible and enabled while the application remains in guest state.
The Notification button is visible and enabled while the user remains unauthenticated.
The Home button is visible and enabled while the application is in Home page guest state.
The Customer signup button is visible, enabled, and available for guest access.
The Clean survey button is visible, enabled, and accessible to unauthenticated users.
The Site survey button is visible, enabled, and accessible in guest state.
The application transitions from Home guest state to the Login page or Login view without authenticating the user.
The application returns to the same Home page guest state with guest controls visible and without displaying authenticated user controls.
The application navigates from the Home guest state to the customer-search page while the session remains unauthenticated.
The application navigates from the Home guest state to the vehicle-survey page without requiring login.
The application navigates from the Home guest state to the survey page while remaining in unauthenticated mode.
The application remains on or returns to the Home page guest state without authenticating the user or hiding guest controls.
The Notification button does not expose authenticated-only functionality and the application remains in guest-accessible state.
The application does not enter an authenticated state and no logged-in user controls become visible after using the Back button.

The Home page displays the Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button as visible and enabled after returning.
The Home page returns to guest state with all approved guest-accessible controls visible and enabled and without authenticated user indicators.
The Home page guest state is restored with approved guest controls accessible and authenticated-only controls still absent.
transitions: The Home page loads successfully at http://localhost/washtabui/home in guest state with guest-accessible controls visible and no authenticated user state displayed.
The Person/Profile button is visible, enabled, clickable, and displayed as part of the Home page guest-state controls.
The Back button is visible and enabled while the application remains in guest state.
The Notification button is visible and enabled while the user remains unauthenticated.
The Home button is visible and enabled while the application is in Home page guest state.
The Customer signup button is visible, enabled, and available for guest access.
The Clean survey button is visible, enabled, and accessible to unauthenticated users.
The Site survey button is visible, enabled, and accessible in guest state.
The application transitions from Home guest state to the Login page or Login view without authenticating the user.
The application returns to the same Home page guest state with guest controls visible and without displaying authenticated user controls.
The application navigates from the Home guest state to the customer-search page while the session remains unauthenticated.
The application navigates from the Home guest state to the vehicle-survey page without requiring login.
The application navigates from the Home guest state to the survey page while remaining in unauthenticated mode.
The application remains on or returns to the Home page guest state without authenticating the user or hiding guest controls.
The Notification button does not expose authenticated-only functionality and the application remains in guest-accessible state.
The application does not enter an authenticated state and no logged-in user controls become visible after using the Back button.

The Home page displays the Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button as visible and enabled after returning.
The Home page returns to guest state with all approved guest-accessible controls visible and enabled and without authenticated user indicators.
The Home page guest state is restored with approved guest controls accessible and authenticated-only controls still absent.

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.

mustNotInvent: duplicate ownership for controls already owned in approved upstream resources
hardcoded workflow routing or mapped page logic in Python
downstreamGuidance.generationRule: Downstream generation must consume approved workflow knowledge plus approved resource context before creating new artifacts.
